attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

During this turn, attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card or a Basic Fighting Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.

You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.  Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon ex.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Riotous Blasting
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon and have this attack do 130 more damage.

attacks.name: Hammer-lanche
attacks.effect: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 100 damage for each Basic Water Energy card that you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Frost Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 160 more damage.

attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

flavorText: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.
Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

attacks.name: Quick Gift
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Due to its short wings, it can't fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

attacks.name: Welcoming Tail
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

attacks.name: Mountain Breaker
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.
Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

attacks.name: Shadowy Side Kick
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

attacks.name: Horrifying Bite
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

flavorText: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

attacks.name: Damage Beat
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A strange Pokémon—it flies without moving its wings, has a hollow shell for a body, and does not breathe.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

attacks.name: Bring Down
attacks.effect: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Water Shot
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.

attacks.name: Aqua Launcher
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clawitzer can move through the water at a speed of 60 knots by using gas inside its body to expel water from the back of its claw.
During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.
When Litleo are young, female Pyroar will teach them how to hunt. Once the Litleo mature, they will leave the pride and set out on their own.
If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so quick, it is said to be able to avoid any attack. It loves to feed on tree sap.
The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stomping Wood
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is called the Walking Jungle. Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.
The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more strength wells up within it, allowing the bud on its back to grow larger.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: For some time after its birth, it uses the nutrients that are packed into the seed on its back in order to grow.

rules:
Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.  Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may switch their Active Water Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Water Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Devolve 1 of your evolved Psychic Pokémon by putting any number of Evolution cards on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can't evolve this turn.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Whenever any player puts a Basic non-Darkness Pokémon onto their Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

During this turn, attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

During your opponent's next turn, all of your Metal Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Each player's Grass Pokémon can evolve into Grass Pokémon during the turn they play those Pokémon, except during their first turn.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card or a Basic Fighting Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.  Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon ex.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Hyper Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.
The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.
Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

attacks.name: Dashing Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Once hot seasons are over, Lopunny's coat will be replaced with fur that holds a lot of insulating air in preparation for colder weather.

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

attacks.name: Bellyful of Milk
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

attacks.name: Quick Gift
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

attacks.name: Rapid-Fire Combo
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.effect: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Carrying food through Fearow's territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Due to its short wings, it can't fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.
It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Illusory Impulse
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: All-You-Can-Grab
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

attacks.name: Windup Swing
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 less damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.
This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.
It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

attacks.name: Chrono Burst
attacks.effect: You may shuffle all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck and have this attack do 80 more damage.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

attacks.name: Gobble Down
attacks.effect: This attack does 80 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Huge Bite
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 30.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Welcoming Tail
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

attacks.name: Miraculous Paint
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

attacks.name: Greedy Hunt
attacks.effect: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.
Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Mountain Breaker
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

attacks.name: Terminal Period
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 6 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Claw of Darkness
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard a card you find there.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cutting Riposte
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for Darkness.

flavorText: Loving to battle, this Pokémon pinches all Pokémon that enter its territory with its pincers and throws them out.
Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey's body.
It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

attacks.name: Stony Kick
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon's habitat.

attacks.name: Shadowy Side Kick
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.
Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Aura Jab
attacks.effect: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mega Brave
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Mega Brave.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Accelerating Stab
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Accelerating Stab.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Cosmic Beam
attacks.effect: If you don't have Lunatone on your Bench, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.
It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

attacks.name: Wild Press
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

flavorText: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.
It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

attacks.name: Pow-Pow Punching
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: This earnest Pokémon battles all sorts of opponents, searching for the fighting style that suits it best.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It is adept at attacking with the spines on its back and its sharp claws while quickly scurrying about.
It digs deep burrows to live in. When in danger, it rolls up its body to withstand attacks.
This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

attacks.name: Horrifying Bite
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

flavorText: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Geo Gate
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Psychic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bright Horns
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Bright Horns.

flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.
It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

attacks.name: Damage Beat
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A strange Pokémon--it flies without moving its wings, has a hollow shell for a body, and does not breathe.

attacks.name: Overflowing Wishes
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mega Symphonia
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

attacks.name: Powerful Hand
attacks.effect: Place 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Alakazam wields potent psychic powers. It's said that this Pokémon used these powers to create the spoons it holds.
This Pokémon's telekinesis is immensely powerful. To prepare for evolution, Kadabra stores up psychic energy in the star on its forehead.

attacks.name: Teleportation Attack
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Abra can teleport in its sleep. Apparently the more deeply Abra sleeps, the farther its teleportations go.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

attacks.name: Electrified Incisors
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, whenever they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, place 8 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: It's one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.

attacks.name: Flash Ray
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Riotous Blasting
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon and have this attack do 130 more damage.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

attacks.name: Electro Fall
attacks.effect: If you have at least 4 Lightning Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

attacks.name: Upper Spark
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Magneton during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Flashing Bolt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Flashing Bolt.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.
The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

attacks.name: Freezing Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: On hot days, these Pokémon press their ice cube heads together and pass the time cooling each other down.

attacks.name: Chilling Wings
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

attacks.name: Bring Down
attacks.effect: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Water Shot
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It extends its long, slimy tongue at blinding speeds and finishes off its prey with great skill.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It gets berries to eat by shooting them down with bullets of water it spurts from its mouth. Its aim is perfect.

attacks.name: Aqua Launcher
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clawitzer can move through the water at a speed of 60 knots by using gas inside its body to expel water from the back of its claw.
This Pokémon launches water by detonating gas inside its right claw. It snipes flying Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hammer-lanche
attacks.effect: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 100 damage for each Basic Water Energy card that you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Frost Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

attacks.name: Riptide
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Basic Water Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Swirling Waves
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It came from overseas. It is a very hardy creature that will quickly proliferate, even in polluted streams.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.

attacks.name: Scorching Earth
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you do, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

attacks.name: Coiling Crush
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.
It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

attacks.name: Turbo Flare
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Although Cinderace loses its cool easily, it will battle flawlessly for a Trainer it trusts.

attacks.name: Jumping Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Thanks to its soft and fluffy fur, Raboot can easily heat up its fire energy and produce flames with more power.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Once its body has heated up, Scorbunny can use the full extent of its power. That's why it does warm-up exercises.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Backfire
attacks.effect: Put 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.
When Litleo are young, female Pyroar will teach them how to hunt. Once the Litleo mature, they will leave the pride and set out on their own.

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 160 more damage.

attacks.name: Volcanic Meteor
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

attacks.name: Supernatural Shapeshifter
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck, and if that card is a Supporter card, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

flavorText: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.
If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

attacks.name: Earthen Power
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: After lowering its anchor, it waits for its prey. It catches large Wailord and drains their life-force.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so quick, it is said to be able to avoid any attack. It loves to feed on tree sap.
It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.

attacks.name: Reversing Gust
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is said that when Shiftry flaps its leafy fans atop ancient trees, chilly winds blow and the season rolls into winter.
It leads a quiet life deep in the forest. If anything wanders into its territory, Nuzleaf will warn the intruder by sounding its grass flute.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: To expand its habitat, it pretends to be a nut so that bird Pokémon will pick it up and carry it far away.

attacks.name: Traverse Time
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Grass Pokémon and Stadium cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon traveled through time to come from the future. It bolsters grass and trees with its own strength, and it can heal wounds, too.
The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
Anyone who stands beside it becomes refreshed, just as if they were soaking in the soothing atmosphere of a forest.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: The spicy scent that wafts from around Bayleef's neck somehow makes those who smell it want to fight.
It loves to bask in the sunlight. It uses the leaf on its head to seek out warm places.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pumped-Up Whip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: Vine growth is accelerated for Tangrowth living in warm climates. If the vines grow long, Tangrowth shortens them by tearing parts of them off.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stomping Wood
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is called the Walking Jungle. Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.

attacks.name: Jam-Packed
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Grass Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If you touch one of Exeggcute's heads, mistaking it for an egg, the other heads will quickly gather and attack you in a swarm.

attacks.name: Jungle Dump
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

flavorText: The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more strength wells up within it, allowing the bud on its back to grow larger.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: For some time after its birth, it uses the nutrients that are packed into the seed on its back in order to grow.
